title: Bank of America Q1 2026 Earnings Highlights
text: Bank of America reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $8.6 billion, up 17% from the year-ago quarter, and diluted EPS of $1.11, up 25%. Revenue, net of interest expense, rose 7% year over year to $30.3 billion, supported by higher net interest income, sales and trading revenue, asset management fees, and investment banking fees. Net interest income increased 9% to $15.7 billion, helped by market activity, higher deposit and loan balances, and fixed-rate asset repricing. Consumer Banking generated $3.1 billion of net income and $11.0 billion of revenue, while Global Wealth and Investment Management generated $1.3 billion of net income and $6.7 billion of revenue. Global Markets net income was $2.0 billion, with sales and trading revenue of $6.4 billion, including a 30% increase in equities revenue. The company returned $9.3 billion to shareholders through dividends and repurchases and reported a CET1 ratio of 11.2%.
